title: Adding guest users via API that don't already exist
excerpt:

We’re looking to use Monday.com for collaboration with clients.  What we are trying to do though is have a strict schema of fields and in doing so, it means our team that are using the board won’t be board owners, j…

posts.author: matcamp.alab (Mat Camp)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

We’re looking to use Monday.com for collaboration with clients. What we are trying to do though is have a strict schema of fields and in doing so, it means our team that are using the board won’t be board owners, just members (as we don’t want them to add new columns or vary existing ones etc as this will flow into pre-built reporting across multiple boards).

The problem then comes around how they add our clients to the board as guest users. I was hoping to use the API as we have a no code platform that we use to build simple interfaces to manage other tools. From what I can see though, you can only add users via the API as owner or subscriber, and not kind of guest.

I really want to keep that board structure locked to minimise structural changes, but it doesn’t seem like I can add guests any other way than to enable board membership?

Please tell me I’m missing something and there is a way to do this!!

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @matcamp.alab

There are no API calls to create new members to your account. Please add it as a feature request.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there!

I have turned this into a feature request
